# ADR-003 Compliance — Globalization & Localization

**Governance phase:** 3.5 · **Authority document:** `docs/ADR-003-*.md` · **Date:** 2026-07-19
**Verifier:** `architecture/compliance/verify-architecture.mjs` · **Do not edit the ADR itself.**

## Purpose
Turn 'country' from assumption into architecture: locale-driven i18n with fallback chains, RTL-native, plus configuration cascade and compliance model.

## Scope
Business globalization architecture — localization model, config cascade (Global→Country→Market→City→Zone), Jurisdiction/compliance. No code prescription.

## Current implementation status
**Certified (business); Localization layer realized for Users — 35% compliant.**

## Implemented components
- Locale VO + supported-locale set (ar,en) with ar default
- Message catalog with fallback chain (unknown locale→ar, unknown code→code)
- Accept-Language negotiation wired into Users controller via DI
- RTL/Arabic first-class (default locale)

## Remaining work
- Extend localization to Identity + remaining controllers
- Country/currency/tax configuration cascade
- Jurisdiction/compliance rule families
- Locale persistence (no users.locale column yet)

## Evidence
- Users A/B 17/17 (ar byte-identical)
- live i18n proof: ar identical / en localized / fr→ar fallback
- tests/unit/localization.test.js

## Related source code
- `src/domain/localization/*`
- `src/application/localization/index.js`
- `src/domain/users/profileValues.js`

## Related tests
- `tests/unit/localization.test.js`

## Compliance percentage
**35%** — honest estimate: weight of ADR requirements realized under the ADR-005
layered architecture with evidence, vs. total requirements named in the ADR.

## Risks
- Identity still hardcodes Arabic (inconsistent surface)
- No config cascade yet — currency/tax hardcoded to Kuwait

## Required actions
- Extend catalog + negotiation to Identity next
- Add users.locale column under ADR-004 before persisting locale
